sentence: It is _____ to park here. You will get a fine.
hint: Think about the negative prefix for words starting with L
explanation: LEGAL → illegal (prefix il-: not legal)

sentence: The instructions were _____. Nobody could understand them.
hint: Think about the negative prefix meaning 'not'
explanation: CLEAR → unclear (prefix un-: not clear)

sentence: She was _____ to finish the project on time.
explanation: ABLE → unable (prefix un-: not able)

sentence: I need to _____ my essay before submitting it.
explanation: WRITE → rewrite (prefix re-: to write again)

sentence: The company decided to _____ the old building.
explanation: BUILD → rebuild (prefix re-: to build again)

sentence: I _____ with your opinion on this matter.
explanation: AGREE → disagree (prefix dis-: the opposite of agreeing)

sentence: The old bridge has _____. We need a new one.
hint: The opposite of appear, in past tense
explanation: APPEAR → disappeared (prefix dis-: to stop appearing, + past tense -ed)

sentence: This chair is very _____. You can sit in it for hours.
explanation: COMFORT → comfortable (suffix -able: having the quality of comfort)

sentence: The food at that restaurant is always _____.
explanation: ENJOY → enjoyable (suffix -able: capable of being enjoyed)

sentence: Is this water _____? I am very thirsty.
explanation: DRINK → drinkable (suffix -able: capable of being drunk)

sentence: The _____ of the city has grown a lot in recent years.
explanation: POPULAR → popularity (suffix -ity: the state of being popular)

sentence: We need to use _____ when we drive.
explanation: ELECTRIC → electricity (suffix -ity: the quality or state)

sentence: The _____ of the situation made everyone nervous.
explanation: SERIOUS → seriousness (suffix -ness: the quality of being serious)

sentence: His behaviour was completely _____. I could not believe it.
hint: Not acceptable — combine a prefix and a suffix
explanation: ACCEPT → unacceptable (prefix un- + suffix -able: not capable of being accepted)

sentence: The damage to the car was _____. It could not be fixed.
explanation: REPAIR → irreparable (prefix ir- + suffix -able: not capable of being repaired)

sentence: She showed great _____ during the competition.
explanation: CREATIVE → creativity (suffix -ity: the quality of being creative)

sentence: The weather is very _____ this week. It changes every hour.
explanation: PREDICT → unpredictable (prefix un- + suffix -able: not capable of being predicted)

sentence: They plan to _____ the kitchen next summer.
explanation: DECORATE → redecorate (prefix re-: to decorate again)

sentence: The _____ of the new product was a great success.
explanation: AVAILABLE → availability (suffix -ity: the state of being available)

sentence: He felt a sense of _____ after losing the match.
explanation: DISAPPOINT → disappointment (suffix -ment: the state of being disappointed)

sentence: The old sofa was very _____. We had to replace it.
explanation: COMFORT → uncomfortable (prefix un- + suffix -able: not having comfort)

sentence: She made a _____ recovery after her illness.
explanation: REMARK → remarkable (suffix -able: worthy of being remarked upon)

sentence: The _____ of the team depends on good communication.
explanation: ABLE → ability (suffix -ity: the quality of being able)

sentence: I was _____ with the service at the restaurant.
explanation: SATISFY → dissatisfied (prefix dis- + suffix -ed: not satisfied)

sentence: The _____ of the old factory created many new jobs.
explanation: DISCOVER → discovery (suffix -y: the act of discovering)

sentence: We need to _____ the importance of education.
hint: To find something for the first time
explanation: COVER → discover (prefix dis-: to uncover or find)
